self_fixes_deferred.deferred_reason: monitor/prompts/*.md is git-owned; edits require clone-based commit+push. Decider 403 rate is currently 100% on every run, which means tinker-side commits would also 403 without an alternative push path. Workspace-sync's universal-pusher does not push prompts (correctly). Filing as operator action #1 instead.
self_fixes_deferred.self_fix_eligible_when: Either (a) operator restores reliable push from scheduled-task sessions, or (b) policy explicitly authorizes workspace-sync to push monitor/prompts/*.md when tinker authored them.

recommendations.description: Apply the 5 stranded EXP patches (EXP-255 hero scaffolding, EXP-256 falsifiability module, EXP-257, EXP-261 corrected hero, EXP-264 evaluate-preface) to build-scripts/generate-html.js in a clean clone, run `node build.js publish`, and close ISS-1605. The workspace already has these applied — diff against main to recover the patch content if the EXP files alone are ambiguous. Each cron tick of decider that does NOT push deepens the drift.

Decide on a structural fix for the persistent decider 403. Options: (a) document the env quirk and accept universal-pusher rescue is the steady-state for runtime data + manual operator action for NEVER_PUSH files; (b) extend universal-pusher to also push tinker-authored prompt edits and decider-authored build-scripts edits when explicitly tagged 'rescue-allowed'; (c) investigate the scheduled-task session difference vs. interactive Cowork (per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001 the PAT itself is fine). Without a structural fix, ISS-1605-class issues will keep recurring whenever an EXP touches build-scripts/.

Update CLAUDE.md `Monitoring Pipeline` table and tinker.md dispatcher table to match actual cron: dome-analyst hourly bumped (currently 50 * * * *), dome-curmudgeon 4h drain (20 */4 * * *), dome-decider 2h (15 */2 * * *), dome-workspace-sync hourly (0 * * * *). Replace the 'Variable (30m / 8h)' / '(1h / 8h)' shorthand with the actual schedule plus a note that BAU varies by drain/churn-and-burn mode. This finding has now persisted in 3 consecutive tinker reports.

Add a step to decider.md that updates priority-queue.json schedule_state when the actual cron diverges from the recorded interval. The decider is the sole writer; it can detect divergence via `list_scheduled_tasks` and self-correct. Currently the field has been stale 10 days — analyst/curmudgeon=30m, decider=60m, last_schedule_change=2026-04-17 — while actual is 60/240/120. Without an amendment this will keep showing up.

Close PROP-005 as wontfix-archived (decider no-op rate self-resolved via drain mode; original 42% no-op problem doesn't reproduce; 6 consecutive STILL_BROKEN runs). Close PROP-011 similarly if it's superseded by integration_drift_alert workflow. Re-evaluate PROP-008 and PROP-012 once curmudgeon exits drain mode — both were authored for hourly curmudgeon and may be unnecessary at 4h cadence.
